interventions.title: United States of America: DOE announces funding to support domestic critical mineral and material supply chains
interventions.description:
On 19 May 2026, the U.S. Department of Energy's (DOE) Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation announced USD 19.3 million in grants to USA Rare Earth as part of the Critical Material Innovation, Efficiency, and Alternatives funding program. The funding will support the construction and operation of a pilot-scale continuous ion exchange (CIX) rare earth element production plant in Stillwater, Oklahoma.

The funding forms part of a broader DOE announcement of USD 45.7 million for 19 projects. The stated purpose of the measure is to address domestic critical minerals and materials supply chain gaps and reduce reliance on foreign sources of rare earth elements. The announcement notes that more than 95% of the U.S. supply of rare earth elements comes from foreign sources and frames the investment as a means to strengthen national security and energy independence.

interventions.title: Canada: Canada Growth Fund provides CAD 145 million in convertible notes to Sayona Inc for lithium mine expansion
interventions.description:
On 12 May 2026, the Canada Growth Fund (CGF) announced a commitment to invest up to CAD 145 million (approximately USD 105.43 million) in Sayona Inc to support the expansion of the North American Lithium (NAL) mine in Québec. The funding is provided via convertible notes and forms part of a larger CAD 417 million capital raise intended to enhance cost competitiveness and increase production capacity at Canada's largest operating lithium mine.

The financing is expected to support 252 jobs in the Abitibi region. A portion of the committed capital is contingent on the company meeting specific milestones, including permitting progress and expansion de-risking.

In this context, Yannick Beaudoin, President and CEO of CGF Investment Management Inc, stated that the project "reinforces Canada’s role in the North American battery materials value chain and supports jobs and industrial competitiveness in Québec." Lucas Dow, Managing Director and CEO of Sayona's parent company Elevra Lithium Limited, noted that the capital "helps de-risk the staged expansion and gives us greater certainty to deliver growth at NAL."

interventions.title: Argentina: RIGI benefits approved for "Expansion Phase 1B" project from Minera del Altiplano SA Sucursal Dedicada
interventions.description:
On 6 April 2026, the Ministry of Economy of Argentina published Resolution No. 431/2026 approving the "Expansion Phase 1B" as a beneficiary of the Incentive Regime for Large Investments (RIGI in Spanish). The project will be managed by Minera del Altiplano SA Sucursal Dedicada, a dedicated mining branch focused on lithium carbonate production. Under RIGI, the project will obtain tax benefits for five years. The effective inclusion date is 25 March 2026, hence the tax benefits will apply retroactively.

The tax benefits include a 5-year cap on the Income Tax of 25% (the normal rate progressively increases until 35%), and tax credits applicable to the VAT. Additionally, Minera del Altiplano SA Dedicated Branch will be able to import certain goods exempted from import duties and internal taxes (see related interventions).

The project is located in the Salar del Hombre Muerto, Catamarca Province, and involves expanding the installed lithium carbonate production capacity from 28'500 tonnes per year to 38'000 tonnes per year. According to the Resolution, the project will "expand the installed capacity of lithium carbonate production by nine thousand five hundred (9'500) additional tons per year".

interventions.title: France: Orano Hydro Battery Recycling receives EUR 82 million grant from the EU's Innovation Fund
interventions.description:
On 18 March 2026, the European Commission approved a EUR 82 million (USD 94 million) financial grant for Orano Hydro Battery Recycling. The grant was provided for the company’s involvement in the “REC2pCAM” project in France, selected as a beneficiary of the EU’s Innovation Fund. Orano Hydro Battery Recycling is a subsidiary of the Orano group dedicated to recovering and purifying valuable materials from end-of-life electric vehicle batteries.

According to the project description, the REC2pCAM project aims to build an industrial-scale facility in France dedicated to producing high-quality precursor cathode active material (PCAM) for nickel-cobalt-manganese (NCM) batteries using entirely European-sourced recycled battery materials. At full capacity, the plant is expected to produce around 20'000 tonnes of PCAM annually, strengthening the European battery supply chain, reducing reliance on imported critical raw materials, and supporting the deployment of low-emission electric vehicles.

The EU's Innovation Fund

Funded by revenues from the auction of emission allowances from the EU’s Emissions Trading System, the Innovation Fund provides financial grants for the demonstration of innovative low-carbon technologies. It is exempted from state aid rules.

Spain: EUR 200 million grant to expand EV battery, energy storage, and hydrogen manufacturing capacity
Canada: Government provides CAD 21.9 million to Mangrove Lithium for spodumene processing and lithium conversion project
Brazil: FINEP announces BRL 200 million grant scheme for mineral transformation and urban mining
United States of America: EXIM approves loan to establish a Strategic Critical Minerals Reserve
interventions.description:
On 2 February 2026, the US government, through the Export-Import Bank of the United States, approved a Direct Loan of up to USD 10 billion to support Project Vault in the United States. The financing aims to establish a Strategic Critical Minerals Reserve to secure access to essential raw materials for domestic manufacturers. The intervention targets US-based manufacturing supply chains and seeks to support production, processing, and storage of critical minerals across multiple states.

The approved loan will provide long-term financing to Project Vault, an independently governed public-private partnership involving original equipment manufacturers and private capital providers. According to the announcement, participating manufacturers reportedly include Clarios, GE Vernova, Western Digital, and Boeing, while suppliers include Hartree Partners, Mercuria Americas, and Traxys. The reserve is expected to store critical raw materials in facilities distributed across the United States to mitigate supply disruptions.

In this context, EXIM Chairman John Jovanovic said: “Project Vault is designed to support domestic manufacturers from supply shocks, support U.S. production and processing of critical raw materials, and strength America’s critical minerals sector. Thanks to President Trump’s leadership, the U.S. Strategic Critical Minerals Reserve will help manufacturers in the United States compete, grow, and lead globally while creating jobs domestically, strengthening our economy, and advancing the national interest.”

interventions.title: Canada: Canada Growth Fund provides USD 65 million in structured financing to Mangrove Lithium
interventions.description:
On 15 January 2026, the Canada Growth Fund (CGF) provided a USD 65 million structured financing package to Mangrove Lithium. The CGF did not specify the form of financing.

The state aid will support the commercialisation and deployment of an electrochemical lithium refining platform. Mangrove Lithium is a British Columbia-based cleantech company that has developed an electrochemical lithium refining technology to produce battery-grade lithium hydroxide and carbonate with lower carbon intensity than conventional methods, currently operating a 1'000-tonne facility and planning a 20'000-tonne Eastern Canada plant.

In this context, François-Philippe Champagne, Minister of Finance and National Revenue, said: "Investing in Canadian cleantech is an investment in our economic sovereignty, and is crucial to building a competitive, low-carbon economy. The funding announced today for Mangrove Lithium will help create here at home a future where innovation thrives, jobs are created, and global competitiveness is advanced".

Canada Growth Fund is a CAD 15 billion arm's-length investment vehicle that uses financing instruments to catalyse private capital investment in Canadian low-carbon projects, technologies, businesses, and supply chains.

interventions.title: Australia: Government increases Critical Minerals Facility budget to AUD 5.2 billion
Russia: Government exempts companies from the mineral extraction tax under certain conditions
interventions.description:
On 17 February 2023, the president of Russia signed federal law No.29 zeroing the mineral exploration tax (MET) for the companies engaged in MET of nickel, copper, and platinum-group metals in the Krasnoyarsk region. 

Specifically, the law makes the tax calculation coefficient zero from 1 January 2026 to 1 January 2038 for projects satisfying specific requirements stipulated by the law. The list includes the conclusion of an investment agreement with the Ministry of Finance and the Ministry of Natural Resources on the extraction of platinum group metals in the area. The local media reported that the firm eligible for the tax exemption is Russian Platinum, a Russian holding focused on producing platinum-group metals.

Previously in 2021, Russian Platinum's project in the region received government support in the form of the VEB.RF credit (see related state act).

interventions.title: United States of America: Department of War discloses funding to expand germanium and silicon optics production
interventions.description:
On 22 December 2025, the US Department of War announced providing USD 18.5 million under Defense Production Act Title III to Lattice Materials. The funding supports expanded production of germanium and silicon crystals at the company’s Bozeman, Montana facility. The announcement did not specify the type of funding.

According to the announcement, the funding targets the US defense industrial base and aims to strengthen the optics supply chain supporting multiple military platforms. The funding reportedly increases capacity for optical-grade germanium and silicon crystal growth and optics manufacturing. It also establishes domestic capability to produce germanium metal from recycled scrap. The funding seeks to reduce lead times for infrared lenses, windows, and mirrors used in surveillance, reconnaissance, and targeting systems.

In this context, Assistant Secretary of War for Industrial Base Policy Mike Cadenazzi said: “Mitigating supply chain vulnerabilities and expanding domestic production for critical minerals is one of DOW's highest priorities. This investment will help ensure a secure supply of germanium and silicon-based optics for surveillance, reconnaissance, and targeting capabilities that our warfighters need."

interventions.title: United States of America: U.S. Administration awards Crucible Metals LLC USD 210 million in funding for smelter and critical mineral processing facility in Tennessee
Canada (Ontario): Government launches a CAD 500 million Critical Minerals Processing Fund
United States of America: DOE announces funding to support rare earth element supply chains
interventions.description:
On 1 December 2025, the United States Department of Energy’s Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation (CMEI) announced a funding opportunity of up to USD 134 million to strengthen domestic supply chains for rare earth elements (REEs). The initiative supports projects that can demonstrate the commercial viability of recovering and refining REEs from unconventional feedstocks, such as mine tailings, e-waste, and other waste materials. Funding will be provided through cooperative agreements and is open to, among others, for-profit entities.

According to the announcement, this funding opportunity aims to strengthen domestic supply chains by reducing U.S. reliance on foreign sources of rare earth elements. Its objectives are to:

• Support the design, construction, and operation of cost-effective demonstration processing lines at a new or existing facility capable of supplying 150–1000 metric tons per year of rare earth elements for domestic use;

• Require a single-site facility that can process, separate, and refine rare earth oxides into rare earth metals from feedstocks such as acid mine drainage, mine waste, deleterious materials, or e-waste, with priority given to heavy rare earth elements.

Funding will be provided through cooperative agreements and is open to, among others, for-profit entities. Individual awards are expected to range between USD 67 million and USD 134 million, with 1–2 awards anticipated. Applicants must provide a minimum 50% cost share of total project costs.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Energy Chris Wright said: “For too long, the United States has relied on foreign nations for the minerals and materials that power our economy. We have these resources here at home, but years of complacency ceded America’s mining and industrial base to other nations. Thanks to President Trump’s leadership, we are reversing that trend, rebuilding America’s ability to mine, process, and manufacture the materials essential to our energy and economic security.”

A cooperative agreement is a federal funding mechanism similar to a grant but includes more active government participation, offering both financial support and technical guidance throughout the project period.

interventions.title: Russia: Government announces RUB 4 billion in grants to Amur Minerals LLC and Udokan Copper LLC
interventions.description:
On 24 November 2025, the government of Russia published Order No. 3351-р allocating up to RUB 2 billion (USD 24.7 million) in financial grant to Amur Minerals LLC, a Russian mining company developing the Malmyzhskoye copper-gold deposit and constructing an integrated mining.

According to the government order, the subsidy will finance the technological connection of the project to the regional power infrastructure operated by Rosseti. This support forms part of the federal project “New Opportunities for the Far East” under the state programme “Socioeconomic Development of the Far Eastern Federal District.” Funding is intended to reduce capital costs for critical infrastructure and help the company meet technical requirements necessary to bring the mining complex into full-scale operation.

In this context, Prime Minister Mikhail Mishustin stated [translation]: “Such measures are necessary to encourage investors to increase capacity and launch new businesses in this strategically important macro-region for the entire country.”

India: Incentive scheme announced for recycling critical minerals
France: Government announced EUR 200 million in tax credits for Imerys' lithium project
Australia: ARENA announces AUD 500 million Battery Breakthrough Initiative to support and grow domestic battery manufacturing
interventions.description:
On 19 August 2025, the Australian Renewable Energy Agency (ARENA) allocated AUD 500 million (USD 323 million) under the Battery Breakthrough Initiative (BBI) to support projects through capital grants. Funding for these projects will also be provided via production incentives (see related intervention).

According to the announcement, the funding will be used for three key areas of battery manufacturing:

  • Active materials (projects for cathode, anode and electrolyte production),
  • Battery cell manufacturing (projects supporting manufacture of battery cells and components)
  • Battery pack assembly (projects related to connecting cells and modules into battery packs).

The programme is also intended to provide clean energy manufacturing opportunities for Australia’s workforce and allow the country to leverage its expertise in battery energy storage.

In this context, ARENA CEO Darren Miller said: “Energy storage is a critical enabling technology for our renewable energy future. We know that solar and wind energy can provide us the lowest cost renewable energy generation at scale, but we also need to be able to store it for when it is needed.”

“BBI will enhance Australia’s battery manufacturing capability, improve supply chain resilience and battery manufacturing processes, and foster innovation.”

Notably, ARENA was launched in 2011 and counted with a total budget of AUD 2.5 billion (USD 1.82 billion) that were to be used until 30 June 2020. The institution received additional funding in 2020 (please see related state act). The new budget allocated to ARENA has extended the existence and budget of this institution, providing it with a total budget of AUD 6.02 billion (over USD 4.3 billion) to finance projects related to renewable energy and energy efficiency.

The Australian Renewable Energy Agency (ARENA)

ARENA is a publicly financed corporation which, among other duties, provides, funding for research in clean energy, the development, deployment and commercialization of renewable energy, and the storage and dissemination of information related to clean energy.

interventions.title: Germany: Government granted EUR 103.6 million in financial support to Vulcan Energy for lithium extraction and processing projects
interventions.description:
On 22 July 2025, the Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy (BMWE) announced a financial contribution of EUR 41.44 million (USD 49 million), corresponding to 40% of the total public funding of EUR 103.6 million, to support two investment projects for lithium extraction and processing in Germany. These projects are co-financed with the states of Rhineland-Palatinate and Hesse, each contributing 30% of the total amount (see related interventions). The type of financial support that will be provided was not specified.

According to the federal government, the funding supports the combined development of lithium extraction in Landau and the further processing of lithium chloride into lithium hydroxide monohydrate in Frankfurt-Höchst. The projects are led by subsidiaries of Vulcan Energy Resources GmbH, namely Natürlich Landau Lithium GmbH and Vulcan Projektgesellschaft 2 GmbH, operating in Landau and Frankfurt-Höchst, respectively.

The processed product, lithium hydroxide monohydrate, is described as a key starting material for modern lithium-ion batteries and supports electromobility and energy storage applications.

interventions.title: Germany: Government granted EUR 103.6 million in financial support to Vulcan Energy for lithium extraction and processing projects
interventions.description:
On 22 July 2025, the Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy (BMWE) announced that the government of Hesse would contribute EUR 31.08 million (USD 36.4 million), representing 30% of the total public funding of EUR 103.6 million, to support a lithium processing project within its territory. The type of financial support that will be provided was not specified.

According to the project description, the funding supports the construction of a conversion plant in Frankfurt-Höchst by Vulcan Projektgesellschaft 2 GmbH, a subsidiary of Vulcan Energy Resources GmbH. This plant will further process the lithium chloride produced in Landau into lithium hydroxide monohydrate.

interventions.title: Germany and Bulgaria: EIB and Aurubis AG signed an investment loan for driving recycling and copper production
interventions.description:
On 17 July 2025, the European Investment Bank (EIB) and Aurubis AG signed a EUR 200 million (USD 232 million) loan. The loan will finance strategic projects to expand copper refining at its Bulgarian site and scale up metal recycling and environmental protection at its Hamburg plant. Aurubis AG is a German supplier of non-ferrous metals and a copper recycler.

According to the press release, this five-year loan reinforces sustainable metal production and supports European Union policy objectives such as economic growth, regional development, and sustainable business practices. It represents the first EIB financing for the sector since the adoption of the new EIB Group strategy to secure Europe’s access to critical raw materials, actively supporting the rollout of the Critical Raw Materials Act.

In this context, Nicola Beer, Vice President of the European Investment Bank, noted: “Securing critical raw materials like copper and expanding recycling activities in Europe are essential to Europe’s resilience, sustainable development, and industrial competitiveness. Copper is the hidden backbone of our modern lives — it keeps the lights on in our homes and powers the smartphone in our pockets, while also driving innovation in electric cars and renewable energy.”

The EIB is the EU's main lending bank, owned by the EU member states. The EIB provides long-term financing for projects that contribute to EU policy objectives, including climate action, digitalisation, infrastructure development, and support for mid-caps and SMEs.

interventions.title: Canada: CIB provides a CAD 55 million loan to Torngat Metals' rare earths project
interventions.description:
On 23 June 2025, Canada Infrastructure Bank (CIB) provided a CAD 55 million (approx. USD 40.12 million) loan to Torngat Metals. Torngat Metals is developing the Strange Lake rare earth minerals project in Nunavik and Sept-Îles.

The loan supports the next phase of the project, enabling essential infrastructure work such as utility connections, airstrip rehabilitation, and improvements to existing facilities to prepare for the future development of an open-pit mine and concentration plant. This financing also aims to bridge project financing gaps and mitigate risks associated with early-stage mining development in Canada's critical minerals sector.

In this context, Ehren Cory, CEO of Canada Infrastructure Bank, said: "The CIB is pleased to provide a project finance loan to Torngat Metals to advance the next phase of the Strange Lake mining project - our first in the critical minerals sector. We are proud to partner with the private sector to catalyze investment in rare earths and advance nationally significant projects that strengthen Canada’s position in global exports markets."

Canada Infrastructure Bank

Canada Infrastructure Bank (CIB) is a federal financial agency investing in revenue-generating infrastructure across Canada. The CIB partners with private, public, and institutional investors to support its projects. It employs multiple funding tools including equity, loans, and derivatives.

interventions.title: Morocco: Government imposes temporary export licensing requirement on certain copper and aluminum alloys
China: Government announces export control measures for various items related to rare earths
interventions.description:
On 4 April 2025, the Chinese Ministry of Commerce adopted Announcement 2025/18, announcing export control measures on various items related to certain medium and heavy rare earths. The measure is implemented from the day of publication. 

Specifically, the announcement covers items related to samarium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, lutetium, scandium, and yttrium. The announcement specified 33 HS codes at the 10-digit level. 

For all items in the announcement, exporters need to apply for special export licenses and fill in documentation for dual-use items and technologies. The Ministry of Commerce takes a decision based on the application documents. Exporting without a licence results in penalties and a possible further investigation. 

The stated objection is to "safeguard national security and interests and fulfill international obligations such as non-proliferation".

On the same day, the Chinese government adopted additional measures, including additional tariffs on imports from the US and additions of US companies to its Unreliable Entity List and its Export Control List (see related state acts).

interventions.title: South Africa: Industrial Development Corporation provides a ZAR 800 million loan to Palabora Copper (Pty) Ltd
interventions.description:
In March 2025, the Industrial Development Corporation (IDC) of South Africa disclosed it had provided a general loan of ZAR 800 million (USD 42.72 million) to Palabora Copper (Pty) Ltd. The beneficiary is a copper mining and processing company. The company produces refined copper products such as electrolytic tough pitch (ETP) copper rod, as well as by-products from copper refining including anode slimes (rich in precious and rare metals), sulphuric acid, magnetite, vermiculite, and nickel sulphate crystals.

The loan was disclosed in the IDC-Funded Business Partner List for the financial year 2024/25, which in South Africa ends on 31 March 2025. In the absence of a specified date for the loan, the end of FY 2025 was selected as an inception date.

Industrial Development Corporation (IDC)

The Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa (IDC) is a state-owned development finance institution that provides funding and support to grow industrial capacity, promote economic diversification, and drive job creation in South Africa and the wider region.

interventions.title: South Africa: SARS introduces export permit requirements for certain copper products
interventions.description:
On 7 March 2025, the South African Revenue Service mandated export permits for certain tariff headings related to copper products. This requirement applies to 7403.12, 7403.13, 7403.19, 7403.21, 7403.22, and 7403.29.

Export permits can be granted by the International Trade Administration Commission of South Africa (ITAC).

interventions.title: Canada: Authorities allocated funding worth CAD 20 million towards Critical Elements Lithium Corporation
interventions.description:
On 6 February 2025, the Canadian government provided state aid worth CAD 20 million (approx. USD 14 million) to Critical Elements Lithium Corporation. The funds are expected to assist the firm's construction of an electrical station to service its lithium-tantalum mining project in Quebec. The state aid is provided under the Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund (CMIF) and is announced pending final due diligence. The government did not specify the form the state aid would take. 

Five other firms also received funding, however, these did not meet the GTA reporting criteria.

In this context, the Honourable Jonathan Wilkinson, Minister of Energy and Natural Resources said: " These projects, under the Canadian Critical Minerals Strategy, will help expand Quebec’s sustainable critical minerals production, notably rare earths that are used in electronics, clean energy, aerospace, automotive and defence. Developments like this help mines get built faster, and they are a key element in seizing the generational opportunity before us. The Government of Canada is supporting projects that strengthen Canada’s supply chains, enhance our ability to be a reliable supplier of the critical minerals the world is demanding and foster economic growth while creating good jobs".

Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund 

The Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund (CMIF) is a Canadian federal initiative providing up to $1.5 billion in funding over seven years (2023–2030) to support clean energy and transportation infrastructure projects that enable the sustainable development and expansion of critical minerals. The CMIF offers two avenues for support: grants for Indigenous engagement and capacity building and contribution funding for preconstruction activities and shovel-ready infrastructure projects.

Australia (Western Australian): Authorities announce AUD 140 million in funding and incentives to support the lithium sector
Australia (Western Australian): Authorities announce AUD 140 million in funding and incentives to support the lithium sector
interventions.description:
On 27 November 2024, the Western Australian government announced an AUD 50 million (approx. USD 32.5 million) temporary interest-free loan scheme to support lithium miners and help them sustain their operations.

The loans will be interest-free until the average lithium spodumene prices exceed USD 1'100 per tonne for two quarters or by 30 June 2026, whichever is earlier.  The companies will be required to repay the loans within two years after the end of the interest-free period.

The West Australian government also announced a temporary waiver on government fees for lithium mining firms (please see related intervention). Finally, the West Australian government also announced a waiver on port charges and mining tenement fees, however, this did not meet the GTA reporting criteria. 

In this context, Premier Roger Cook stated: "This package will provide important temporary and responsible support for WA's fledgling lithium industry, taking into account the extremely challenging market conditions it is facing. This package is aimed at helping our critical lithium industry and its workforce to the other side of this turbulent period - supporting local jobs and doing what's right for WA".

interventions.title: Canada (Quebec): Government announced CAD 250 million in state aid to Nemaska ​​Lithium
United States of America: Department of Energy announces a USD 2.3 billion loan to Lithium Nevada Corp for the construction of a lithium carbonate processing plant
interventions.description:
On 28 October 2024, the U.S. Department of Energy’s (DOE) Loan Programs Office (LPO) announced a loan for Lithium Americas Corp’s subsidiary, Lithium Nevada Corp. The aid will support the construction of a lithium carbonate processing plant in Nevada. The total value of the funding is USD 2.26 billion.

According to the announcement from LPO, Lithium Nevada will build a lithium carbonate processing plant at Thacker Pass in Humboldt County, Nevada. The facility is planned to produce around 40’000 metric tonnes per year of battery-grade lithium carbonate for use in lithium-ion batteries. This is equivalent to batteries for over 800'000 electric vehicles (EVs) annually.

In this context, Jonathan Evans, President and Chief Executive Officer of Lithium Americas said: "The United States has an incredible opportunity to lead the next chapter of global electrification in a way that both strengthens our battery supply chains and ensures that the economic benefits are directed toward American workers, companies and communities."

Funding is announced through the DOE’s Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing (ATVM) program. Authorized by the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007, ATVM supports the manufacturing of eligible vehicles and qualifying components and materials that improve fuel economy. A conditional loan commitment is an offer approved by the DOE to extend a loan to a project, contingent upon the project meeting specified technical, legal, environmental, and financial conditions before the DOE finalizes the financing agreement and disburses the funds. A conditional loan commitment is offered following the completion of the due diligence process.

On 30 September 025, the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) announced that its Loan Programs Office had restructured its 2024 loan agreement with Lithium Americas Corp (see related state act).

interventions.title: United States of America (State of Michigan): Approval of funding for semiconductor-grade polysilicon production
interventions.description:
On 21 October 2024, the Michigan Economic Development Corporation announced financial support for Hemlock Semiconductor to support its semiconductor-grade polysilicon production. The total value of funding is USD 40 million and it will be provided in the form of grants.

According to the announcement, this funding is in addition to the proposed USD 325 million in federal support from the Commerce Department under the CHIPS and Science Act. Hemlock Semiconductor, one of the world’s largest producers of hyper-pure polysilicon and the only one headquartered in the United States, plans to expand its production capacity of semiconductor-grade polysilicon to meet the demands of leading-edge chip applications.

In this context, Governor Gretchen Whitmer said: “Today, cutting-edge Michigan company Hemlock Semiconductor secured a proposed $325 million investment from the Biden-Harris administration to create 180 good-paying jobs and build a new facility to help make polysilicon, a critical material that’s found in nearly every electronic device in the world. HSC is one of many Michigan-based companies that is helping us lead the future of advanced manufacturing. Thanks to the Biden-Harris administration’s bipartisan CHIPS and Science Act and the U.S. Department of Commerce, we are ensuring HSC’s global leadership in the powerful domestic supply chain to manufacture semiconductor chips needed for everything from computers and phones to AI applications. Together, we are strengthening national and economic security by ensuring supply chains, good-paying jobs, and cutting-edge investments are where they belong—here at home. Let’s build the future we all want—one where America leads.”

Funding was announced through the ‘Make it in Michigan Competitiveness Fund’. Launched in May 2023, the Fund was designed to enhance Michigan's economic growth by securing federal investments from legislations such as the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law, the CHIPS and Science Act, and the Inflation Reduction Act. The fund provides matching grants to projects that require non-federal contributions, aiming to unlock federally backed investments in target sectors (see related state act).

interventions.title: United States of America: DOE announces USD 150 million in funding for ABTC to support the production of battery materials
interventions.description:
On 20 September 2024, the US Department of Energy announced that it would award American Battery Technology Company (ABTC) USD 150 million in funding to support the construction of a commercial-scale lithium-ion battery recycling facility in South Carolina. Funding will be provided in the form of a grant.

According to the announcement, ABTC will process approximately 100'000 tonnes of battery materials annually from its battery manufacturer and automotive OEM partners, producing battery-grade minerals such as nickel, cobalt, manganese, copper, aluminium, graphite, and lithium hydroxide for resale.

The funding was announced as part of the DOE’s Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing program, which supports retrofitting, expanding, and building new domestic facilities for battery-grade critical minerals, components, manufacturing, and recycling. The funding opportunity (FOA) of this program was issued in November 2023 (see related state act) and represents the second FOA under the program; the first FOA was announced in May 2022 (see related state act) with awardees selected in October 2022. Including this award, DOE announced approximately USD 3 billion for 25 projects across 14 states to increase the domestic production of advanced batteries and battery materials.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm said: “We’re in the midst of a manufacturing revival in the United States as the Biden-Harris Administration’s Investing in America agenda continues to breathe new life into communities and local economies across the country. Russia: Government allocates RUB 4 billion for the construction of the energy infrastructure for the new mineral processing plant
interventions.description:
On 19 September 2024, the government of Russia allocated RUB 4 billion (USD 42.2 million) to support the construction of the energy infrastructure for the new mineral processing plant at the Ak-Sug copper-porphyry deposit in the Republic of Tyva. According to the government, the new plant will be constructed by an unspecified private investor. 

Specifically, the government will fund the construction of overhead power lines and other energy infrastructure facilities needed to ensure the work of the enterprise. According to the press release, the government is financing the construction of this energy infrastructure to support this particular investment project. 

In this context, the Head of the Russian government Mikhail Mishustin said [translation]: "The government will allocate 4 billion rubles to implement investment projects for the extraction and processing of non-ferrous metals in the Republic of Tyva, which has significant raw material reserves. This will increase the attractiveness of this sector for business, help develop large deposits, support the construction of new mining and processing plants, and give additional impetus to the development of the entire region."

interventions.title: Japan: Government announces a JPY 1.5 billion financial grant to Nippon Chemical Industry Co Ltd to test the recycling of critical minerals
interventions.description:
On 10 September 2024, the government of Japan announced approximately JPY 1.5 billion (USD 10.54 million) in support of the critical minerals supply security plan of Nippon Chemical Industry Co Ltd. The aid will take the form of a financial grant.

According to the plan, "demonstration will be carried out at a pilot plant to recover and refine nickel, cobalt, and lithium from black mass produced in the lithium-ion battery recycling process."

Nippon Chemical Industry Co Ltd is a Japanese company specialising in the production and development of chemical products, including synthetic resins, adhesives, and functional materials for various industrial applications.

The measure's stated objective is to ensure a stable supply of critical minerals. The support is granted under Japan's Economic Security Promotion Act.

interventions.title: Finland: Finnvera signs EUR 250 million loan guarantee for Keliber Technology Oy's lithium project
interventions.description:
On 26 August 2024, the Finnish export credit agency Finnvera signed a EUR 250 million (USD 279.4 million) loan guarantee with Keliber Technology Oy. The guarantee will support the development of Keliber's lithium project in Kaustinen and Kokkola, Finland.

Keliber Technology Oy is a Finish mining and chemical company that produces battery-grade lithium hydroxide for electrification and energy storage. 

According to the press release, this type of instrument is granted to a "Finnish domestic investment that promotes exports. The Finnish interest in the Keliber project is strong, the procurements are almost entirely of domestic origin and the Finnish company Metso supplies the key technology of the project."

interventions.title: Finland: EIB provides financing for 'Keliber Battery Grade Lithium Production'
interventions.description:
On 20 August 2024, the European Investment Bank (EIB) and Keliber Oy signed a state loan agreement worth EUR 270 million (approx. USD 297 million) for the 'Keliber Battery Grade Lithium Production' project from Finland. The total cost of the project is estimated at EUR 723 million.

The EIB issued the following project description: 'Mining and production of battery grade lithium (Li) hydroxide. The project comprises the successive development and mining of several Li-mineral ore mines, a concentrator plant to produce an ore concentrate and an innovative first-of-a-kind hydro-metallurgical processing plant to produce battery grade lithium hydroxide (LiOH) with a nominal capacity of 15 kt/a'.

The objective of the project is defined as follows: 'The product is intended as a high-grade lithium hydroxide mainly for the Li-ion battery cell manufacturing industry in Europe'.

A state act in the GTA database is assessed solely in terms of the extent to which its implementation affects foreign commercial interests. On this metric, the investment support granted here is discriminatory.

interventions.title: Turkiye: Ministry of Industry and Technology announces a funding call worth USD 4.5 billion to support the production of battery cells and active materials
interventions.description:
On 26 July 2024, the Turkish Ministry of Industry and Technology announced a tax relief measure to support businesses investing in the production of battery cells and active materials. In addition to the tax relief, the Turkish government has announced a comprehensive package of incentives, including free land allocation, financial grants, state loans, and equity support (see related interventions). These combined incentives will be supported by a total budget of USD 4.5 billion.

According to the announcement from the Ministry, this funding call aims to increase Türkiye's production capacity to meet domestic demand and capitalize on the export potential, particularly to the European market. The goal is to establish a total production capacity of 80 GWh, focusing on local content and including investments in R&D centres. The call will support investments in battery and active material production facilities with a minimum annual capacity of 5 GWh, starting from the cell level. The tax relief for each company can be up to 80% of its investment value.

Funding was announced as part of the HIT-30 High Technology Investment Program, a government initiative aimed at attracting high-value-added investments in key technology sectors. This funding call is one of six announced by the Ministry for different priority sectors (see related state acts). It is stated that more funding calls will be released under the HIT-30 program.

In this context, Minister of Industry and Technology Mehmet Fatih Kacır said: “Under the HIT-30 High Technology Incentive Program, we aim to attract global investments to Turkey, especially in the fields of innovative emerging technologies. Through the first six calls, we intend to make Turkey a production hub for next-generation energy vehicles, battery technologies, chip technologies, solar cells, and wind turbines. Through these six calls, we will attract more than USD 20 billion in private sector investments to our country by 2030" (author's own translation).

interventions.description:
On 21 June 2024, the government of the Russian Federation announced approximately RUB 1.6 billion (USD 18.17 million) in state aid to Udokan Copper. The government did not specify the form the state aid would take. 

The government funds will support the construction of a mining and processing plant extracting and processing copper ore and the production of cathode copper and commercial sulphide concentrate. This specific allocation will fund part of the main project, namely, "Boiler room No. 1 and related infrastructure". Udokan Copper (formerly Baikal Mining Company) is a firm established in 2008 with the objective of developing the Udokan Copper Deposit in the Kalar District of the Trans-Baikal Region. 

In this context, Mikhail Mishustin, the Prime Minister of Russia, said: “We expect that this decision will facilitate the influx of private investment, primarily in sectors key to economic development, and improve the lives of our citizens in the Far East".

United States of America: Introduction of tax credits worth USD 19.6 million for American Battery Technology Company under the Advanced Energy Project Credit Program
interventions.description:
On 19 April 2024, the Department of Energy awarded American Battery Technology Company an investment tax credit worth USD 19.6 million. This funding will support the company in building a facility for recycling lithium-ion batteries and producing critical materials. The funding was provided through the Advanced Energy Project Credit Program.

According to the company-provided project description, American Battery Technology Company is building a facility for recycling lithium-ion batteries with a capacity of 20'000 tonnes per year. This facility will produce critical materials such as nickel sulphate, cobalt sulphate, and lithium hydroxide, which are reintroduced into the U.S. supply chain.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm said: “Through the historic Advanced Energy Project Credit, we are leveraging the infrastructure, expertise, and grit of America’s energy communities—where the workers that powered our energy past, will power our energy future.”

U.S. Secretary of the Treasury Janet Yellen said: “Investments in advanced energy projects strengthen our energy security and create good-paying jobs in vital fields like clean energy manufacturing and critical materials processing. They also allow for existing energy infrastructure to be upgraded for the clean energy economy. All of this work will help lower energy costs for American families and small businesses.”

Established by the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act and later expanded with a USD 10 billion investment through the Inflation Reduction Act, the Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Credit (48C) Program provides tax credits for investments in advanced energy projects (see related state acts). These projects support clean energy manufacturing and recycling and aim to reduce greenhouse gas emissions at industrial facilities. The program addresses a wide range of needs in the clean energy economy, including the production of grid components, electric vehicle parts, and other key technologies. Concurrent with the present project, the Department of Energy announced funding for 35 projects across four categories: Clean Energy and Clean Vehicle Manufacturing, Critical Minerals and Materials, Grid Components and Modernization, and Industrial Decarbonization, with a total allocation of USD 1.93 billion in tax credits.

In 2024, the Shanghai-listed firm Zijin Mining Group Company Limited (also known as ZIJIN MINING) disclosed in its stock exchange filings the receipt of approximately USD 33 million of government subsidies.

Zijin Mining Group Company Limited is a Gold enterprise based in Longyan, Fujian Province.

In China it is a legal requirement that publicly-listed firms report any subsidies received from government bodies. The stated subsidy amounts refer to a given calendar year. It is possible that this firm received other forms of state aid that have not been declared.

The affected products and sectors have been chosen based on the "main products" and CSRC sector classification reported in the financial data of the firm. The subsidy amounts in USD were computed using the year-average exchange rate to the Chinese Renminbi. The subsidy amount(s) reported here are the cash equivalents as estimated by the recipient firm.

A state act in the GTA database is assessed solely in terms of the extent to which its implementation affects the extent of discrimination against foreign commercial interests. On this metric, the state aid proposed here is discriminatory.

Canada: Launch of the CAD 1.5 billion Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund
Canada: Launch of the CAD 1.5 billion Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund
interventions.description:
On 31 October 2023, the government of Canada launched the CAD 1.5 billion (USD 1.08 billion) Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund (CMIF). The official call for proposals will be published later in autumn 2023. The CMIF will be supporting projects related to clean energy and critical raw materials for 7 years. An unspecified part of the CMIF state aid would come in the form of repayable contributions. 

Specifically, the government will support clean energy projects related to "generation, storage and transmission of non-emitting renewable or alternative energy, as well as grid connectivity" as well as transportation-related projects facilitating "the development or expansion of critical minerals resources". Although the bulk of funding under the CMIF will be provided in the form of non-repayable contributions, some projects will be supported with state loans. 

In addition, the CMIF will also provide state aid in the form of financial grants (see related intervention). 

In this context, Jonathan Wilkinson, Minister of Energy and Natural Resources, said: “Critical minerals are a generational economic opportunity for Canada. As major enablers of clean technologies and clean energy sources, demand for critical minerals is projected to rise exponentially as the global economy continues to shift toward low-carbon solutions. Through the $1.5-billion Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund, Canada will make strategic investments in projects to help enable and grow the sustainable development of these minerals, reinforcing Canada’s position as a global supplier of choice for clean technology, clean energy and the resources the world needs to build a prosperous net-zero economy".

interventions.title: United States of America: Department of Energy provides funding for battery recycling
interventions.description:
On 27 September 2023, American Battery Technology Co (ABTC) announced receiving USD 10 million from the US Department of Energy to demonstrate and commercialize new techniques for lithium-ion battery (LIB) recycling processes for the production of low-cost domestic critical battery materials. Funding was provided in the form of a grant.

According to the announcement from the company, the total value of the project is USD 20 million. DOE will provide USD 10 million in grant funding, while ABTC and its partners will provide an additional USD 10 million as part of the cost-sharing arrangement between ABTC and DOE.

In this context, ABTC CEO Ryan Melsert said: “It is an important milestone to be commissioning the initial portion of our integrated lithium-ion battery recycling facility to be able to provide domestically produced low-cost and low-environmental impact battery metals to our strategic customers. However, any company that is complacent with current technologies will not be competitive in the future, which is why we are also prioritizing our pipeline of new advanced technologies that are being evolved through our research, bench development, piloting and commercialization infrastructure.”

Funding was provided through the Electric Drive Vehicle Battery Recycling and Second-Life Applications program under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (see related state act).

American Battery Technology Company (ABTC) is a provider of critical battery materials for the electric vehicle, electrical grid storage, and consumer electronics industries.

interventions.title: Germany: Financial grant scheme to support research and developments projects related to battery technologies
interventions.description:
On 21 September 2023, the Federal Ministry of Education and Research announced the launch of the "Research and Development on Battery Technologies for Technologically Sovereign, Competitive and Sustainable Battery Value Chains (B@TS)" financial grant scheme. The scheme's stated objective is to support innovation in the field of battery technologies. It is in place from 21 September 2023 until 30 June 2027. The German authorities notified an annual budget of EUR 25 million for the scheme. The total budget, therefore, is EUR 100 million.

More specifically, the scheme supports companies' as well as other entities' (such as research institutes) "project-related activities" in the field of research, development and innovation related to battery technologies. Projects must be located in one of the following fields of action: materials and production process research; scaling research and digitization; resource-saving battery cycles and securing raw materials; promising technology variants of the future; and battery (research) ecosystem. Eligible companies can receive state aid of up to 50% of eligible costs. Grants will be allocated in several rounds of project calls. 

Notably, following Articles 107 and 108 of the TFEU, this type of state aid is covered by the General Block Exemption Regulation (GBER) and therefore does not require a formal decision from the European Commission.

A state act in the GTA database is assessed solely in terms of the extent to which its implementation affects foreign commercial interests. On this metric, the financial support granted here is discriminatory.

interventions.title: United States of America: DOD provides funding to expand domestic lithium mining
interventions.description:
On 12 September 2023, the U.S. Department of Defense (DOD) awarded Albemarle Corporation a USD 90 million contract to increase domestic mining and production of lithium for battery supply chains. The announcement did not specify the type of funding.

According to the announcement, the funding will enable Albemarle Corporation to reopen its Kings Mountain, N.C. lithium mine. The company anticipates the mine to become operational within the timeframe of 2025 to 2030.

In this context, Anthony Di Stasio, DOD MCEIP Director said: “The agreement with Albemarle demonstrates the DoD's ongoing commitment to meeting the needs of our warfighter, today and in the future. This investment directly supports President Biden's April 2022 Presidential Determination for Critical Materials in Large-Capacity Batteries."

For the Presidential determination for critical materials in large-capacity batteries, see related state act.

interventions.title: United States of America: Introduction of funding to strengthen domestic critical minerals and material supply chains
interventions.description:
On 6 September 2023, the US Department of Energy issued a funding opportunity to support the domestic production of critical minerals and materials. Funding is open to, among others, for-profit entities. The total value of the funding is USD 150 million and it will be provided in the form of a cooperative agreement.

The purpose of the funding program is to stimulate research, development, and demonstration (RD&D) activities that will contribute to the establishment of a resilient domestic supply chain for critical minerals and materials essential for clean energy technologies and national security requirements.

The DOE anticipates awarding approximately 25-30 awards, with individual grant amounts ranging from USD 500 thousand to USD 30 million. Furthermore, the program also mandates a cost-sharing arrangement, requiring beneficiaries to fund at least 20% of the total project costs for research and development projects and 50% of the total project costs for demonstration and commercial application projects.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm said: “The President’s Investing in America agenda is ramping up access and use of clean energy technologies which means increased demand for critical minerals and materials. The investments announced today enhance national security by reducing our reliance on foreign sources—while strengthening an existing mining and energy workforce to develop sustainable sourcing for these vital materials right here in the U.S.A.”

Funding is provided through the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (see related state act).

interventions.title: United States of America: Department of Energy provides funding to strengthen the critical minerals supply chain
interventions.description:
On 21 August 2023, the Department of Energy announced the availability of USD 30 million to support the domestic production of rare earths and other critical minerals and materials from domestic coal-based resources. Funding will be provided in the form of cooperative agreements. Eligible beneficiaries include, among others, for-profit entities.

According to a DOE announcement, rare earth elements are found in various sources, including domestic coal and coal wastes. The funding opportunity aims to support projects focused on the research, extraction, separation, and refining of these elements. The goal is to produce rare earths and critical minerals from these unconventional resources, thereby helping to build a domestic supply chain

The funding announcement has two topic areas, namely ‘Advanced Process Development for Production of Rare Earth Metals and Co-Production of Critical Minerals and Materials from Coal-Based Resources’, and ‘Production of Critical Minerals and Materials Excluding Materials Containing Rare Earth Elements from Coal-Based Resources’.

DOE plans to make individual awards ranging from USD 5 million and USD 2.5 million in federal funding depending on the topic area, with a minimum of a 20% cost-sharing from the awardees.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm said: “President Biden’s Investing in America agenda is providing a historic opportunity to convert coal products into the critical materials needed to build an array of clean energy products. The President’s transformative investments will strengthen our national security by lessening our dependence on international supply chains while delivering high-quality jobs and healthier communities for all Americans.”

A cooperative agreement is a funding mechanism used by the federal government to provide financial assistance to organizations to carry out a project. It is similar to a grant. The main difference between a cooperative agreement and a grant is the level of involvement from the federal agency. In a cooperative agreement, apart from providing funding, the government is actively involved in the project, providing technical assistance and guidance.

The funding was provided by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (see related state act). In July 2023, DOE announced another funding opportunity to support critical minerals supply chain from domestic coal-based resources.

interventions.title: United States of America: DOD provides funding to increase production of graphite for large capacity batteries
interventions.description:
On 17 July 2023, the U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) awarded Graphite One a USD 37.5 million contract to increase domestic graphite mining and processing capabilities for large-capacity batteries.

According to the announcement, funding will enable Graphite One to develop a domestic advanced graphite supply chain solution. This includes mining from Graphite Creek and processing the graphite ore through an advanced material and battery anode manufacturing plant. The plan also includes a recycling facility.  

In this context, Assistant Secretary for Industrial Base Policy, Laura Taylor-Kale, said: “This investment to increase domestic capabilities for graphite exemplifies Industrial Base Policy's commitment to building a resilient industrial base to meet current and future national defense requirements. The agreement with Graphite One (Alaska) advances the Defense Department's strategy for minerals and materials related to large-capacity batteries."

interventions.title: United States of America: Department of Energy announces funding to support critical minerals supply chain
interventions.description:
On 13 July 2023, the Department of Energy announced the availability of USD 32 million for projects that will support the production of rare earth elements and other critical minerals from domestic coal-based resources. Funding will be provided in the form of grants. Eligible beneficiaries include, among others, for-profit entities. 

According to the announcement from DOE, rare earth elements naturally occur in many places including in domestic coal and coal wastes. This funding announcement will fund research, development, and demonstration projects that support the development and building of facilities for the production and refining of rare earth elements and other critical minerals across the country.

In this context, U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm said: “President Biden’s Investing in America agenda is providing a historic opportunity to convert coal products into the critical materials needed to build an array of clean energy products. The President’s transformative investments will strengthen our national security by lessening our dependence on international supply chains while delivering high-quality jobs and healthier communities for all Americans.”

The funding was provided by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (see related state act).

interventions.title: Netherlands: Funding for "Material Independence & Circular Batteries" project on battery technology
interventions.description:
On 30 June 2023, the Dutch government announced the allocation of a financial grant amounting to up to EUR 296 million (USD 321.6 million) to the “Material Independence & Circular Batteries” project through the National Growth Fund. The project aims to develop an "ecosystem in parts of the battery chain where the Netherlands can build up a comparative advantage".

More specifically, the project focuses on developing sustainable and circular batteries and consists of 3 programme lines: (1) ensure sustainable material supply (supplying raw materials through refining and battery recycling); (2) development and upscaling of sustainable battery technology; and (3) development of circular battery systems for transport applications and grid stability. The project is proposed by a consortium consisting of more than 60 companies active in the following sectors: battery production, battery materials and components, material refinery, recycling, primary use, as well as reuse and second-use". In addition, knowledge and educational institutions are part of the consortium. BCC–NL, the Dutch Battery Competence Cluster, is the main applicant and leader of the project. 

Notably, out of the total aid amount of EUR 298 million, EUR 118 million has been awarded "conditionally" and EUR 178 million as a "reserve". The beneficiaries are required to meet further conditions before the aid will be allocated to the project.

National Growth Fund

The “Material Independence & Circular Batteries” project is part of the third round of projects receiving funding from the National Growth Fund ("Nationaal Groeifonds", see related state acts). The National Growth Fund has a budget of EUR 20 billion for the period 2021-2025 and should support "projects with the highest potential of contributing to our national earning capacity, or in other words, to durable economic growth".

interventions.title: Australia: The government of Queensland to establish an AUD 100 million Critical Minerals Investment Fund
interventions.description:
On 26 June 2023, the Queensland government launched financial grants under the AUD 100 million (approx. USD 66.8 million) Queensland Critical Minerals and Battery Technology Fund. The fund aims at enhancing the extraction and processing of critical minerals as well as the development of battery technologies so as to support economic growth. The Fund will be administered by the Queensland Investment Corporation (QIC). 

The Critical Minerals and Battery Technology Fund will have two streams of investments namely: financial grants as well as growth capital investments and loans (please see related interventions). 

In this context, Premier Annastacia Palaszczuk said: "Queensland has a clear vision to maximise the momentous opportunities that come with a clean energy future, as outlined in the Queensland Energy and Jobs Plan. Our state’s abundant reserves of vanadium, cobalt, copper and rare earth elements are the key to accelerating the development of clean energy technologies like solar panels, battery storage and electric vehicles".

The Queensland Critical Minerals and Battery Technology Fund was first announced in the month of December 2022.

Canada (Saskatchewan): Government Increases the Saskatchewan Mineral Exploration Tax Credit (SMETC)
interventions.description:
On 27 March 2023, the government of the province of Saskatchewan announced an increase in the Saskatchewan Mineral Exploration Tax Credit (SMETC) rate from 10 to 30 percent. The measure represents higher tax credits for the critical mineral exploration sector. The announcement was made as part of the Saskatchewan Critical Minerals Strategy. 

According to the Critical Minerals Strategy, the tax credit increase will incentivise critical mineral exploration in the province. The government's stated objective is to drive Saskatchewan's share of critical minerals investment from 8.5 percent to 15 percent and to make it "the best place in Canada for mining". 

In this context, Premier Scott Moe said: “Saskatchewan is a critical minerals leader today and will be a critical minerals powerhouse tomorrow. With our existing expertise in mining and processing, our province has the opportunity to become a critical minerals hub and meaningfully contribute to the global demand for these resources".

interventions.title: Canada: Funding for critical minerals development under the Canadian Critical Minerals Strategy
interventions.description:
On 7 March 2023, the government of Canada announced the investment of CAD 144.4 million (around USD 105.03 million) under the Critical Minerals Technology and Innovation Program. The funding will contribute to developing the local critical minerals value chain within the Canadian Critical Minerals Strategy framework. 

The investment in the Critical Minerals Technology and Innovation Program will support "research, development, demonstration, commercialization and adoption of new technologies and processes" by the companies operating in the Canadian critical minerals value chain. The funding announcement confirms the investment that the Canadian government committed to in the 2022 Budget (see related state act). According to the information in the 2022 budget, the government will be providing support within Critical Minerals Technology and Innovation Program for 5 years. 

In this context, Jonathan Wilkinson, Minister of Natural Resources, said: “Canadian innovators are leading the way toward a cleaner future. Today’s investment of $344 million will help to advance the development of a dynamic and competitive critical minerals sector. This means good jobs for workers, more investment in Canadian innovation and lower emissions across the country — all part of our plan to build a cleaner Canada and a prosperous, sustainable economy that works for everyone.”

interventions.title: Canada: Funding for critical minerals development under the Canadian Critical Minerals Strategy
interventions.description:
On 7 March 2023, the government of Canada announced the investment of CAD 79.2 million (around USD 57.82 million) under the Critical Minerals Geoscience and Data Initiative to support critical mineral reserves exploration. The funding will contribute to developing the local critical minerals value chain within the Canadian Critical Minerals Strategy framework. 

The investment in the Critical Minerals Geoscience and Data Initiative will support the development of geoscience and mapping technologies to assist companies in identifying mineral reserves. The funding announcement confirms the investment that the Canadian government committed to in the 2022 Budget (see related state act). According to the information in the 2022 budget, the government will be providing support within Critical Minerals Technology and Innovation Program for 5 years. 

In this context, Jonathan Wilkinson, Minister of Natural Resources, said: “Canadian innovators are leading the way toward a cleaner future. Today’s investment of $344 million will help to advance the development of a dynamic and competitive critical minerals sector. This means good jobs for workers, more investment in Canadian innovation and lower emissions across the country — all part of our plan to build a cleaner Canada and a prosperous, sustainable economy that works for everyone.”

interventions.title: United Kingdom: Introduction of funding to strengthen the supply of critical minerals
interventions.description:
On 27 February 2023, the UK Department for Business and Trade announced the availability of GBP 15 million (USD 17.9 million) to strengthen the supply of critical minerals. Funding will be provided through the CLIMATES programme. The government didn’t specify the type of award instrument.

Funding will support research and development in rare earth elements as well as innovations in the recycling of rare earth elements. Researchers and businesses are eligible for funding.

In this context, Nusrat Ghani, Minister of State at the Department for Business and Trade, said: “The impact of Russia’s illegal war in Ukraine on energy prices has been a timely reminder of how important supply chains are for all parts of our economy. That’s why we are laser-focused on securing robust supply chains, for the growing, green industries that will deliver jobs and prosperity across the UK in the decades to come.”

Mike Biddle, Executive Director for Net Zero at Innovate UK said: “Our CLIMATES programme will work with businesses, investors, and industry bodies to deliver a series of activities, from funding competitions to networking events and workshops, all designed to drive growth in the sector and build resilient supply chains to help us strengthen our economy and meet our environmental targets.”

interventions.title: France: Government provides EUR 40 million loan in favour of Société Le Nickel
interventions.description:
On 13 February 2023, the government of France provided a loan amounting to EUR 40 million (USD 42.8 million) in favour of Société Le Nickel. The loan will support the company in its exploration mission in New Caledonia and its operations in the Doniambo factory. The loan was granted due to rising energy prices and operational issues in the said factory.

Société Le Nickel (SLN) is the New Caledonian subsidiary of the French mining and metallurgy company Eramet. SLN specializes in the exploration, mining, and processing of nickel ore. New Caledonia is a French outermost region.

Switzerland: Adoption of further economic sanctions against Russia, including extended export bans and an investment prohibition on Russian mining projects
interventions.description:
On 25 January 2023, the Swiss Government added 168 Russian entities to the list of entities to which EU operators are prohibited to export dual-use technologies. Specifically, the entities were added to Annex 2 of the Ordinance "On measures related to the situation in Ukraine" of 4 March 2022. The measure enters into force on the same day of its publication.

Specifically, the Swiss operators are no longer allowed to export to the targeted entities dual-use goods and technology as listed in Annex 2 of the Swiss Ordinance "On the Export, Import and Transit of Dual-Use Goods, Specific Military Goods and Strategic Goods" from 3 June 2016 (version of 1 January 2022).

In addition, the Swiss Government adopted further economic sanctions towards Russia, including extended export bans and restrictions on investments in the Russian mining sector (see related interventions). According to the press release, the measures are adopted "in response to Russia's ongoing military aggression against Ukraine and its continued destabilising actions that undermine Ukraine's territorial integrity, sovereignty and security."

Notably, the Swiss Government is adopting the remaining measures set out in the European Union’s ninth package of sanctions of 16 December 2022, of which Switzerland had already partially adopted measures on 20 December 2022 (see related state acts).

interventions.title: Australia: NAIF allocates additional AUD 220 million to Hastings' Yangibana Rare Earth Minerals Project
interventions.description:
On 17 January 2023, the Australian government-funded Northern Australia Infrastructure Facility (NAIF) approved a loan of AUD 220 million (approx. USD 153.7 million) to Hastings Technology Metals Limited, an Australian rare earth company. 

The objective of the financing is to support the Hastings Yangibana Rare Earths Project. The project is expected to deliver a mixed rare earth carbonate product and create close to 700 jobs as well as AUD 1.3 billion in net public benefit.

Previously, the NAIF granted Hastings Technology Metals Limited a loan of AUD 140 million towards the Yangibana Rare Earths Project. (Please see the related state act)

Northern Australia Infrastructure Facility (NAIF):
NAIF is an AUD 7 billion lending facility created by the Australian Government to finance infrastructure projects located in northern Australia or with significant impact for that region, and that are of public benefit and engage the indigenous people (please, see related state acts).

The loans provided by NAIF have a lower interest rate than commercial lenders and borrowing tenors of up to 30 years.

interventions.title: Zimbabwe: Export permits requested for all unprocessed base minerals
interventions.description:
On 6 January 2023, the government of Zimbabwe published Statutory Instrument 5/2023 establishing an export permit for unprocessed base minerals. The measure enters into force immediately. 

Specifically, the measure affects "unbeneficiated" base minerals, i.e. "coal and all other minerals and mineral substances, and includes coke and all such slimes, concentrates, slags, tailings and residues as are valuable and contain base minerals (...), but does not include precious metals, precious stones, mineral oils and natural gases".

The Ministry of Mines and Mining Development will grant the export permit based on two requirements. First, the applicant provided enough proof that the ore cannot be processed anywhere in the country. Second, export samples for assaying purposes after the provision of enough proof that the assaying cannot be done in the country. Violation of the measure will result in a fine twice the value of the base mineral or imprisonment of up to 2 years. 

In December 2022, Zimbabwe established an export ban on unprocessed lithium (see related state act).
